title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are frustrating. To help us narrow this down: when you hit the 403, is it the entire dashboard page that fails to load, or does the page partially load and then some specific panel or data fails? Also, if you open your browser's developer tools (Network tab) and reproduce the error, can you share which specific URL/endpoint returns the 403 and what the response body says? That will help us figure out whether this is an auth/session issue vs. a permissions check on a specific resource.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It's the entire page — when it happens, I just get a full 403 Forbidden page, nothing loads at all. I don't really know how to use the developer tools thing, but I can tell you that when I hit refresh a couple times it usually starts working again. It feels like maybe 1 out of 3 times I get the error? I assumed it was some kind of network issue on our end but my coworker said she doesn't have the problem at all, so now I'm not sure.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful detail, thanks. A couple of things that would help us figure out *why* this is happening to you but not your coworker:

- When the 403 happens, does logging out of TaskFlow and logging back in fix it (as opposed to just refreshing)?
- Are you and your coworker using the same browser, or different ones? And are either of you going through a VPN or corporate proxy to reach TaskFlow?

This will help us tell whether it's a session/authentication issue vs. something about how your traffic is being routed.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I haven't tried the full logout/login thing honestly — I've just been refreshing until it works. I can try that next time it happens. We're both using Chrome and we're both in the office on the same network, no VPN or anything like that. The weird thing is she says she never gets the error. Oh, I just remembered — she's been here longer than me, I don't know if that matters. I was just recently given a new role in the system, the 'analyst' role, like maybe a week or two ago? Not sure if that's related.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: That detail about the new 'analyst' role is really interesting — the timing might line up. Can you recall whether the 403 errors started right around when you were given that role? And does your coworker who doesn't see the error have a different role (e.g., 'admin' or 'editor' rather than 'analyst')? That would help us figure out if this is tied to how the analyst role's permissions are configured.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, I think the 403s started around the same time I got the analyst role, maybe a day or two after? It's hard to say exactly. And yes, my coworker is an editor — she's had that role for a while. So you think it's something wrong with the analyst role permissions? That would make sense I guess, but what's weird is it works sometimes. If it was a permissions thing wouldn't it fail every time?

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Great question — you're right that a straightforward permissions issue would usually fail every time. The intermittent part is the real puzzle, and it's what we need to pin down.

You mentioned at the start that other people on your team are seeing this too. Are those teammates also on the 'analyst' role? And do you know if anyone else with the analyst role does *not* get the error? That would tell us whether this is something about the analyst role's configuration specifically, or something unique to certain accounts.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Now that you mention it, yeah I think the ones who complained about it are also analysts — we were a few of us who got the new role around the same time. I'm not sure if every single analyst has the problem, I haven't asked everyone, but at least two others on my team who got the analyst role said they've seen the same thing. I don't think anyone with admin or editor has mentioned it.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks — that really solidifies the pattern around the analyst role. The remaining mystery is why it's intermittent rather than constant.

Here's something that might help us crack that: next time you get the 403, instead of just refreshing, could you try opening the dashboard in a **private/incognito window** (Ctrl+Shift+N in Chrome) and see if it loads correctly there? And then also try the regular refresh in your normal window.

Also — do you notice any pattern to *when* the 403 hits? For example, does it tend to happen more when you first open TaskFlow in the morning or come back after a break, versus when you're actively clicking around? Or does it feel truly random even during active use?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I can try the incognito thing next time, sure. As for the pattern — honestly it feels pretty random. It's not like a morning thing or anything, it happens throughout the day even when I'm actively working. Like I'll load a page fine, click to another section, and boom 403. Then I refresh and it's fine again. It really does feel like roughly every third time, but I haven't counted exactly. There's no rhyme or reason to it that I can tell.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden on dashboard for users with 'analyst' role","problem":"Users assigned the 'analyst' role receive full-page 403 Forbidden errors on approximately one-third of dashboard page loads. The error occurs across all dashboard sections, not tied to a specific panel or endpoint. Refreshing the page typically resolves it until the next occurrence. The issue began around the time the analyst role was introduced (roughly 1-2 weeks ago). Users with 'admin' or 'editor' roles are unaffected.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The analyst role's permissions are likely being evaluated inconsistently across requests. Most probable causes: (1) The application runs behind a load balancer with multiple backend instances, and one or more instances have a stale or incomplete permissions/RBAC configuration that doesn't fully recognize the analyst role — requests that hit a correctly-configured instance succeed, while those hitting a misconfigured one return 403. (2) There is a caching layer (e.g., session cache, permissions cache) with a short TTL or race condition that intermittently drops or fails to resolve analyst role permissions. (3) The analyst role was added but a related permission grant (e.g., dashboard access scope) was partially applied, and a non-deterministic permission check (such as checking multiple permission sources) sometimes misses it.","reproduction_steps":["Create or use a test account with only the 'analyst' role assigned","Log into TaskFlow and navigate to the dashboard","Repeatedly navigate between dashboard sections or refresh the dashboard page","Within approximately 3-5 attempts, a full-page 403 Forbidden error should appear","Compare behavior with an 'editor' or 'admin' role account — those should never see the 403"],"environment":"Chrome browser, office network (no VPN/proxy), affects multiple analyst-role users on the same team. Issue started approximately 1-2 weeks ago coinciding with the analyst role being granted to a batch of users.","severity":"high","impact":"All or most users with the 'analyst' role are affected. At least 3 confirmed reporters. The issue disrupts normal workflow — users must repeatedly refresh to regain access. No data loss, but significant productivity impact and poor user experience. Workaround exists (refresh the page) but is not acceptable long-term.","recommended_fix":"1. Inspect the RBAC/permissions configuration for the 'analyst' role — verify the dashboard access permission is correctly and completely assigned. 2. If running multiple application instances behind a load balancer, check that all instances have the same permissions configuration (look for config drift or failed deployments). 3. Examine any permission caching layer — check for race conditions, short TTLs, or cache invalidation issues that could cause intermittent permission check failures. 4. Review the authorization middleware/interceptor to see if the 403 decision is logged — correlate 403 responses with the specific permission check that failed and which server instance handled the request. 5. Check if the analyst role was added to the database/config but a dependent migration or cache warm-up was incomplete.","proposed_test_case":"Create an integration test that: (1) assigns the 'analyst' role to a test user, (2) makes 50+ sequential authenticated requests to the dashboard endpoint, (3) asserts that all requests return 200. Run this test against each application instance individually (bypassing the load balancer) to identify if the issue is instance-specific. Additionally, add a unit test for the permission resolver that verifies the analyst role is granted dashboard access under all code paths.","information_gaps":["Which specific authorization check or middleware is returning the 403 (server-side logs not yet examined)","Whether the issue is instance-specific in a load-balanced setup (not yet confirmed if multiple instances exist)","Whether logging out and back in (forcing a fresh session) prevents the 403 vs. just refreshing","Whether incognito mode changes the behavior (could indicate cookie/session-related cause)","Exact server-side error or permission check that fails (reporter was unable to check browser dev tools)"]}
